pattern: Only the open cells connected to a border survive, so flooding inward from the edges marks the safe ones and everything left unmarked is captured.
hints: A region of open cells is captured only if none of its cells touches the grid border, so border-connected open cells and anything reachable from them are safe.
Start a search from every open cell on the four edges, marking all open cells reachable from the border as safe.
Flood-fill (DFS or BFS) from each border 'O', tagging reachable cells with a temporary marker; afterwards turn every remaining plain 'O' into 'X' and restore the markers back to 'O'.

edge_inputs.description: interior region is captured, border-touching region survives
single cell board, nothing to capture
single open cell on the border survives
open region snakes to the border and is fully safe
fully enclosed open block is captured
all open cells, every one touches a border
interior open cell with a border-connected channel stays

stress.description: random grid with mixed cells so captured and safe regions both occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 15)
    n = rng.randint(1, 15)
    board = [['O' if rng.random() < 0.55 else 'X' for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [board]
stress.note: 55 percent open cells gives a healthy mix of border-connected and enclosed regions
